title: Over-Stove Wood Shelf — Simple DIY Range Rack
summary: A small three-board shelf that spans a stove's alcove opening, trim-screwed together in two steps — sized here for a 34" opening.
description:
This is about as small a build as this catalog carries: three boards and ten screws, made to fill the open shelf gap directly above a stove or range. It's sized here for a 34"-wide, 15"-tall opening, which is a common over-range microwave or shelf cutout — measure your own opening before cutting, since this shelf is meant to fit a specific gap rather than a standard furniture dimension.

The build order matters more than the part count suggests: the 1x2 cap attaches to the top of the 1x6 shelf board first, screwed and glued from underneath so the top screws stay hidden. Only after that assembly is together do the two 1x6 side pieces attach to the ends, three trim screws per side. Fill every screw head with wood filler before staining — at this scale, an unfilled screw head is one of the few visible details on the whole piece.

cutList.note: Cut to your opening's actual width.
Cut to your opening's actual width, minus the side pieces' thickness.
Cut to your opening's actual height plus 2-1/4".

steps.title: Attach the top cap
steps.body: Attach the 1x2 top cap on top of the 1x6 shelf board with four trim screws and wood glue, driven from underneath so the screw heads stay hidden.

steps.title: Attach the sides and finish
steps.body: Attach the two side pieces to the ends of the shelf-and-cap assembly with three trim screws per side. Fill every screw head with wood filler, sand smooth once dry, and stain.

images.alt: Small wooden shelf fitted into the open alcove above a stove
